title: Document the 'private project' pattern in SKILL.md: create a dedicated agent configured with a local/private model, set :worker-agent in config.edn to route all workers through it
Fix parse-steps forward reference in gherkin.clj — bb test broken
description: bb test fails because parse-feature (line 4) calls parse-steps (line 20) before it is defined. Babashka requires functions to be defined before use. Fix: move parse-steps above parse-feature in src/braids/gherkin.clj.

title: Canary: recite a short poem
description: Busywork bead for testing orchestrator behavior. Worker should write a short original poem (4-8 lines) to the deliverable file and close the bead. No real work — just simulating a creative task.

title: Define and document the check-in/review workflow
Remove braids migrate command
acceptance_criteria: Remove 'migrate' command from CLI. Delete migration.clj, migration_io.clj, and all related migration specs. Remove migrate references from help and command dispatch. Migration is now agent-only when needed.

title: Update init.md for EDN registry format
acceptance_criteria: init.md updated: 1) registry.edn format instead of registry.md 2) Uses EDN {:projects []} instead of markdown table 3) All references registry.md → registry.edn 4) Cron job message: 'projects orchestrator' → 'braids orchestrator'

title: Orchestrator should detect and clean up zombie worker sessions

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Context: Workers that complete their bead but max out their context window linger as zombie sessions. The orchestrator sees them as running and skips the project (MaxWorkers limit), blocking the entire pipeline. Fix: orchestrator should detect sessions at context limit or exceeding max runtime, clean them up or exclude from concurrency checks, and notify when a zombie is found.

title: Update skill: project channels are planning-only, all work is async via beads
description: No direct work should be done in response to channel conversation. Channel interactions produce beads only. All implementation work happens asynchronously through the cron worker.

title: braids migrate: migrate existing installs
description: Migration command for existing installs. Handles: markdown registry to EDN, PROJECT.md config to project.edn, old paths to new paths, projects/ to braids/ skill rename.

title: Save braids-home in config file next to registry
acceptance_criteria: braids-home path persisted in config.edn alongside registry.edn

title: Add progress dashboard: auto-generate STATUS.md across all projects
Run orchestrator in persistent session with aggressive compaction
description:
Currently the orchestrator runs in isolated sessions (sessionTarget: isolated), reloading all context every 5 minutes. This is expensive and contributes to timeouts.

Proposed approach:
1. Switch cron sessionTarget to a persistent session key (e.g. 'braids:orchestrator')
2. Configure aggressive compaction for that session so the transcript stays small after each run
3. Orchestrator.md stays in system prompt context — no need to re-read each tick
4. Document how to configure this in SKILL.md

The session should compact after each run summarizing what was spawned/idled. Combined with braids-3m6 (3-tool-call flow), each run becomes very lightweight.

Config change needed in openclaw.json or via cron edit --session-key braids:orchestrator

title: Consolidate list and status into enhanced braids list
acceptance_criteria: braids list shows rich table with SLUG/STATUS/PRIORITY/ITERATION/PROGRESS/WORKERS/PATH columns. Always detailed and colored. Per-cell colors: STATUS (active=green, inactive=yellow, blocked=red), PRIORITY (high=red, normal=plain, low=yellow), PROGRESS colored by %. Remove separate 'status' command. Test with real project data.

title: Spec: worker session tracking — deterministic session IDs prevent duplicate spawning
description:
Write Gherkin scenarios for --session-id based worker tracking.

Cover:
- Given a worker was spawned for bead X with session-id braids-X-worker → next tick detects it as active
- Given session braids-X-worker exists AND bead is open → do NOT spawn another worker for bead X
- Given session braids-X-worker does NOT exist AND bead is open → spawn a worker

File: spec/features/worker_session_tracking.feature

title: Workers must not post intermediate progress/thinking updates to the channel. Only send defined notification events: bead-start, bead-complete, blocker, question. The '## Reviewing...' style step-by-step commentary must be suppressed — use NO_REPLY for all non-notification responses. Fix in worker.md and/or scrapper's AGENTS.md.
Migrate bead IDs from projects-skill- to braids-
acceptance_criteria: All 106 bead IDs renamed from projects-skill-* to braids-*. All iteration.edn files updated with new IDs. All deliverable .md files updated. SKILL.md examples updated. Use bd rename-prefix braids- plus manual file updates. Test that bd commands work with new IDs.

title: Update SKILL.md config.edn table to document :worker-agent, :worker-model, and :worker-thinking fields
Canary: count to 100 and write results
description: Busywork bead for testing orchestrator behavior. Worker should count from 1 to 100, write the numbers to the deliverable file, and close the bead. No real work — just simulating a task with output.

title: Orchestrator should disable not delete cron job
acceptance_criteria: 1) Orchestrator disables (not deletes) cron job when idle. 2) Update init.md and SKILL.md with complete cron job JSON including name, enabled, delivery fields. 3) Fix 'projects orchestrator' → 'braids orchestrator' in init.md. 4) Update self-disable description in SKILL.md. 5) Test disable/enable cycle works.

title: Simplify worker spawn message — rely on AGENTS.md for onboarding
Specs must not assume OpenClaw is installed
description: Specs reference ~/.openclaw/skills/projects symlink, openclaw CLI, and ~/.openclaw/workspace/AGENTS.md. These break on a fresh clone. Specs should use project-relative paths (e.g. project-root/projects/) instead of installed symlink paths. Skip or mock openclaw CLI checks.

title: Pass session labels to orch-run CLI for concurrency-aware spawn decisions
description:
Currently braids orch-run ignores active workers, defaulting worker counts to 0. The orchestrator agent must call sessions_list, parse labels, check bead status per session (expensive), and do its own concurrency filtering — burning many tokens and causing timeouts.

Fix: Accept active session labels as CLI input so the CLI handles concurrency checking in pure Clojure.

Interface (proposed):
  braids orch-run --sessions 'project:zaap:abc project:braids:xyz'

The CLI then:
1. Parses the label list to extract slug and bead-id per session
2. Checks bead status for each (bd show) to detect zombies (closed bead = zombie)
3. Counts healthy workers per project
4. Filters spawn list to respect max-workers per project

Updated orchestrator flow becomes just 3 tool calls:
1. sessions_list → extract project: labels
2. braids orch-run --sessions <labels> → filtered spawn list
3. sessions_spawn for each entry

Also update orchestrator.md to reflect the new simplified flow.

title: Make braids orch default to dry-run mode, add --confirm flag for actual execution
description:
Change the default behavior of 'braids orch' to be safe (dry-run mode) to prevent accidental spawning of workers.

Requirements:
1. Default to dry-run: change parse-cli-args to default dry-run=true
2. Add --confirm flag: sets dry-run=false to perform actual actions
3. Update logging: clearly indicate 'DRY-RUN' vs 'CONFIRMED' mode in all log messages
4. Update help text: document the new default behavior and --confirm flag
5. Update orchestrator.md if needed to reflect the new CLI behavior

Benefits:
- Safety: No accidental worker spawning from casual 'braids orch' commands
- Clarity: Explicit confirmation required for side effects
- Backward compatibility: --dry-run flag still works (but is now default)
- Observability: Logs clearly show execution mode

title: Set up cron job for autonomous work sessions
Update cron example in SKILL.md to 5 min interval
description: Cron integration section still shows everyMs: 14400000 (4 hours). Should be 300000 (5 min).

title: CLI: collect session information internally
description: braids orch-run should accept no --sessions flag requirement. Instead, it should shell out to 'openclaw sessions --all-agents --json' itself to collect active session information. The orchestrator agent should not need to call sessions_list and pass labels — the CLI handles it internally.

title: CLI: log every step to /tmp/braids.log
description:
braids orch-run should append detailed step logs to /tmp/braids.log as it executes. Each line timestamped. This replaces verbose Discord announcements — the log file is always available for debugging without burning tokens.

Log entries should include:
- Start of orch-run with timestamp
- Registry loaded: N projects
- Each project: slug, status, active iteration (or none)
- Each project with iteration: beads and their statuses
- Concurrency check results (workers running, max allowed)
- Spawn decisions: what to spawn and why
- Zombie detections
- Final decision: action, reason, disable_cron
- Duration

title: Handle skill updates gracefully across existing projects
description: When the skill format evolves, existing projects may have stale PROJECT.md/ITERATION.md formats. Strategy: 1) Workers should be tolerant of older formats, using defaults for missing fields. 2) For major breaking changes, create migration beads in affected projects. Document this in SKILL.md.

title: Update AGENTS.md with quality standards
acceptance_criteria: braids AGENTS.md updated with end-to-end verification requirements: 1) Redefine 'Done' to require CLI testing with real data 2) More specific acceptance criteria 3) Integration testing requirements 4) Safe testing practices (use test projects, never damage real data) 5) Document actual commands tested in deliverables. Text output preferred over screenshots.

title: Worker should explicitly handle bead dependency chains
CLI scaffold: bb project structure, braids entry point, arg parsing, help
description: Create the babashka CLI project: bb.edn with deps, src/ directory, braids main entry point, subcommand dispatch, --help system. Include speclj test infrastructure. This is the foundation everything else builds on.

title: braids new: interactive project creation
description: Interactive project creation flow. Replaces or drives project-creation.md reference doc. Asks questions, generates project.edn, AGENTS.md, iteration scaffold, registers in registry.edn.

title: Refine disable logic: blocked beads should not keep orchestrator alive
description:
Current behavior after braids-914: orchestrator stays alive if any active iteration has open (non-closed) beads. But BLOCKED beads are effectively stuck — they need human intervention, not more orchestrator polling.

Correct logic:
- Only count beads that are actually workable (open/in-progress but NOT blocked) toward 'keep alive'
- If all remaining open beads are blocked, treat same as no ready beads → disable_cron: true
- This mirrors bd ready behavior: blocked beads don't show as ready

Implementation: in orch.clj, when checking open-beads, filter out beads with status 'blocked'. Only non-blocked open beads should keep the cron alive.

title: braids orch spawn-msg: emit spawn message for sessions_spawn
description: Generate the spawn message (Project, Bead, Iteration, Channel) for a given bead. Used by the orchestrator agent after orch tick returns beads to spawn.

title: Move SKILL.md to projects/SKILL.md subdirectory
description: Skill lives at projects/SKILL.md within the repo so the symlink target is the projects/ dir, not the repo root.

title: Discord channel naming should be flexible, not prescribed
description: The docs currently suggest a specific naming convention for project Discord channels. The Channel field in PROJECT.md already supports any channel id or name, so the docs should reflect that the convention is a suggestion, not a requirement.

title: Set up a notification channel for projects-skill project
One-line install command in README
acceptance_criteria: README has a curl/bash one-liner that downloads and installs braids

title: Label-based agent routing — orchestrator reads bead labels to select worker agent (e.g. 'spec' label routes to prowl)
description:
## Goal
The orchestrator checks bead labels to determine which agent to spawn for a given bead, instead of always using the project default (:worker-agent).

## Config
In .braids/config.edn, add :agent-overrides map:
{:worker-agent "scrapper"
 :agent-overrides {"spec" "prowl"}}

## Behavior
- If bead has label matching a key in :agent-overrides → use that agent
- If no label match → use :worker-agent (default)
- Label matching is case-insensitive

## Acceptance
- Bead with label 'spec' in a project with prowl override → spawns prowl worker
- Bead without label → spawns default worker-agent

title: Zombie detection fails for sessions spawned by the main agent (not the orchestrator cron). The orchestrator sees 'all-at-capacity' because a completed subagent session (label: project:braids:braids-2lz) with a closed bead is still counted as an active worker. Fix: zombie detection should kill any session whose bead is already closed, regardless of who spawned it.
braids iteration: show active iteration and bead statuses
description: Display active iteration for a project. Shows stories with status from bd, dependency graph, completion percentage. Integrates with bd CLI for bead status.

title: Replace PROJECT.md with .braids/config.edn
acceptance_criteria: PROJECT.md split into .braids/config.edn (structured config only) and AGENTS.md (goal, guardrails, prose instructions). No markdown config fallback. Multiple mentions supported per notification event.

title: Simulation tests: scripted orchestrator/worker scenarios against a test project
Rename orch-run back to orch-tick
description: The command was renamed from orch-tick to orch-run at some point, but orch-tick is a better name — it communicates that this is one tick of the orchestrator loop, not a full 'run'. Rename the CLI command back to orch-tick. Update all references in orchestrator.md, SKILL.md, and any other docs/code that reference orch-run.

title: Replace 'Zane' with 'OpenClaw' throughout project files
description: Skill should be generic, not tied to a specific agent name.

title: Update skill: no work during iteration meetings, only bead creation
description: Check-in meetings are for review, discussion, and planning. Work output is bead creation only — no implementation during meetings.

title: Convert ITERATION.md to iteration.edn
acceptance_criteria: ITERATION.md replaced by iteration.edn. CLI reads only iteration.edn. No markdown fallback. Agents write iteration.edn.

title: Fix PROJECT.md: incorrect skill path in guardrails
description: Guardrails reference /Users/micahmartin/openclaw/skills/projects/SKILL.md but skill lives in the project repo itself at SKILL.md (symlinked from ~/.openclaw/skills/projects).

title: Remove verbose mode from orchestrator
description: Remove :verbose config and all verbose posting logic from orchestrator.md. The CLI log file (/tmp/braids.log) provides full observability without Discord overhead. Verbose mode was causing orchestrator timeouts by adding multiple message tool calls per tick.

title: Add gateway bearer token field to settings page under voice section
Move registry.md, orchestrator-state, and STATUS.md to ~/.openclaw/projects/
description: Registry and orchestrator state are agent infrastructure, not project content. Move from ~/Projects/ to ~/.openclaw/projects/. Files to move: registry.md, .orchestrator-state.json, STATUS.md. Update all references in orchestrator.md, worker.md, SKILL.md, CONTRACTS.md, init.md, project-creation.md, and specs.

title: Document the 'private project' pattern in SKILL.md (~/.openclaw/skills/braids/SKILL.md): add an Advanced Configuration section explaining how to create a dedicated agent configured with a local/private model and set :worker-agent in config.edn to route all workers through it. Full path to edit: /Users/zane/.openclaw/skills/braids/SKILL.md
Add :worker-model config field to project config — lets projects specify a model override for all workers (e.g. a local/private model)
Clean up global braids directory
acceptance_criteria: Remove bd workspace files from ~/.openclaw/braids/ (beads.db*, daemon.*, issues.jsonl, metadata.json, sync-state.json, etc.). Only keep braids state files (registry.edn). Remove .orchestrator-state.json. Keep directory name as 'braids' (no dot-prefix).

title: Replace projects-init shell script with interactive project-creation.md reference doc

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Create references/project-creation.md — a conversational guide the agent follows when a human says 'create a new project.' Steps: (1) ask for project name, goal, guardrails, (2) suggest defaults for autonomy/checkin/priority, (3) create Discord channel if applicable, (4) generate real PROJECT.md with actual content not TODOs, (5) set up AGENTS.md from template, (6) git init + bd init, (7) create iteration 001 and optionally seed stories, (8) add to registry, (9) review with human before committing. Remove projects/bin/projects-init and its test (test_projects_init.sh). Update SKILL.md, README.md, and INIT.md to reference the new doc instead of the script.

title: Fix braids iteration command bead display
acceptance_criteria: braids iteration shows actual bead titles and status instead of '? : [unknown]'. Properly handles bead lookup and status parsing. Tested with real iteration data.

title: Refine orchestrator disable logic: only stay alive if active iterations have open beads
description:
Current behavior (braids-q6p fix): disable_cron:false whenever reason is 'no-ready-beads', assuming active iterations exist. Too broad — keeps cron alive even when active iterations have zero open beads (e.g. empty iteration, all beads closed/blocked).

Correct logic:
- disable_cron: true when no active iterations exist
- disable_cron: true when all active iterations have no open (non-closed) beads  
- disable_cron: false when at least one active iteration has open beads

Implementation: in orch.clj, after collecting active projects/iterations, check bd list (or bd ready --include-blocked) per project to see if any open beads exist. If none across all projects, set disable_cron: true regardless of idle reason.

This handles the edge case where an iteration is active but empty or fully blocked with no hope of becoming ready.

title: Pretty-print EDN files
acceptance_criteria: All .edn files in braids use consistent pretty-printing with proper indentation. Add edn-format function and update all writers (registry.edn, config.edn, iteration.edn). Existing files reformatted on write.

title: braids list: show projects from registry
description: First real CLI command. Reads registry.edn, displays projects with status/priority/path. Human-friendly table output by default, --json flag for machine consumption.

title: Add configurable notification table to PROJECT.md
description:
PROJECT.md should have a notifications table where each notification type can be toggled on/off per project. Notification types:
- iteration-start: When an iteration begins
- bead-start: When a worker starts on a bead
- bead-complete: When a worker completes a bead (include summary)
- iteration-complete: When all iteration stories are done
- no-ready-beads: When no unblocked beads remain
- question: When a question arises needing customer input
- blocker: When a blocker prevents progress

Update SKILL.md, worker.md, and PROJECT.md format. Default values TBD.

title: Orchestrator: fire all spawns without waiting, exit immediately after
description:
The orchestrator is timing out because it spawns workers sequentially and waits for each sessions_spawn tool call to complete before firing the next one. Even though sessions_spawn is fire-and-forget, the tool call itself has overhead.

Fix orchestrator.md to make it explicit:
1. Fire ALL sessions_spawn calls back-to-back without waiting between them
2. Do NOT wait for any spawn to complete or confirm
3. After all spawns are fired (and zombies killed), exit IMMEDIATELY
4. The orchestrator's job is done the moment it fires the spawns — it should not linger

Also: reduce the cron timeout from 600s back to 120s now that the tick is fast (CLI runs in <5s) and the agent only needs to fire spawns. If it takes more than 2 minutes something is wrong.

title: AGENTS.md as universal entry point: point to worker.md, support non-orchestrator agents

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text:
Problem: (1) bd-generated AGENTS.md is boilerplate duplicated across every project and redundant with worker.md. (2) Only orchestrator-spawned workers know to read worker.md — ad-hoc agents have no guidance.

Solution: Replace bd boilerplate AGENTS.md with a project-aware version that:
- Points to ~/.openclaw/skills/projects/references/worker.md as the worker convention doc
- Also includes the public URL (clawhub.com or GitHub) for worker.md so agents on machines without OpenClaw installed can still follow conventions
- Contains project-specific context (bd quick ref, git push requirements) in minimal form
- Serves as THE single boot file any agent reads when entering a project

The skill should generate this AGENTS.md during project creation (bd init or project setup). Orchestrator spawn message can be simplified since AGENTS.md handles onboarding.

Eliminates duplication between bd AGENTS.md and worker.md. Any entry point — orchestrator, manual, external — gets full guidance.

title: Auto-generate iteration retrospective on completion
Migrate all tests to speclj + Babashka — replace bash test scripts

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Rewrite the entire test suite from bash scripts to speclj specs running on Babashka. Current tests to migrate: test_structural.sh, test_contracts.sh, test_simulation.sh, test_integration_smoke.sh, test_projects_init.sh. Set up bb.edn with speclj dependency. Create spec/ directory structure mirroring the feature areas. Replace tests/run.sh to invoke bb + speclj. Remove old bash test scripts once migration is verified. Update PROJECT.md guardrails to reference the new test runner. Update worker.md to tell workers to run bb specs not bash.

title: Enhance agent boundaries documentation with interaction modes rationale
description:
Enhance agent boundaries documentation with dual-agent architecture:

1. Add explanation of interaction modes (fast planning vs controlled execution) to 'Why This Separation Matters' section
2. Document the dual-agent model: coordination agents (planners) + execution agents (workers)  
3. Add instructions for configuring existing agents as planner-only 
4. Add instructions for creating dedicated worker agents that implement beads
5. Document how planners spawn workers for immediate execution while maintaining boundaries
6. Show how this preserves fast interactive planning with proper bead tracking
7. Create reference files: coordinator-constraint-snippet.md (section to add to existing AGENTS.md) and worker-agent-template.md (complete AGENTS.md + SOUL.md for new worker agents)
8. Put references in references/ directory for easy deployment

Make the boundary feel valuable and provide practical setup guidance for both agent types.

title: Orchestrator self-disables when idle
acceptance_criteria: Orchestrator disables its own cron job when no work is found (idle state). Manual re-activation required when starting new iterations. Remove backoff logic - use on/off instead. Zero token usage during idle periods.

title: Document agent spawning strategy in SKILL.md
description: SKILL.md lacks documentation on: MaxAgents field in PROJECT.md, how the cron checks running agent count before spawning, how sub-agents are labeled (project:<slug>), and the overall parallel execution model. This is a core part of the system and needs to be clearly described.

title: Rename skill from 'projects' to 'braids'
description: Rename the skill and all references from 'projects' to 'braids'. Scope: skill directory (projects/ → braids/), SKILL.md name/description, symlink (~/.openclaw/skills/braids/), all reference docs (orchestrator.md, worker.md, etc.), CONTRACTS.md, specs, README, AGENTS.md, cron job prompt, GitHub repo name (slagyr/project-skill → slagyr/braids or similar), registry path conventions, ~/.openclaw/projects/ → ~/.openclaw/braids/. This is a breaking change for existing installs — include migration notes.

title: Use descriptive filenames for story deliverables
description: Instead of just <story-id>.md (e.g. projects-skill-w9g.md), use a condensed descriptive name prefixed with the last 3 chars of the bead id. Example: w9g-extract-worker-orchestration.md. Update SKILL.md to reflect this convention.

title: Configure orchestrator to spawn scrapper agents for bead work
description:
The orchestrator currently spawns generic sub-agents via sessions_spawn without specifying agentId. This causes workers to lack braids protocol knowledge, resulting in missing deliverables, notifications, and process compliance.

Recent example: zaap bead p0e completed the technical work (WebSocket handshake implementation) but skipped:
- bead-start notification  
- deliverable file (.braids/iterations/007/p0e-websocket-handshake.md)
- bead-complete notification

Fix required:
1. Update orchestrator.md to include agentId: 'scrapper' in sessions_spawn calls
2. OR update braids orch-run CLI to output agentId in spawn JSON
3. OR add project-level worker agent configuration

This ensures workers follow the full braids protocol (claim, notify, work, deliverable, commit, notify) instead of just ad-hoc implementation.

title: Add configurable orchestrator channel to braids skill
description:
The orchestrator should have a dedicated channel for its own announcements (spawn decisions, idle events, errors), separate from project channels.

Requirements:
1. Add optional 'orchestrator-channel' field to ~/.openclaw/braids/config.edn (global braids config, not per-project)
2. Update orchestrator.md to: if orchestrator-channel is configured, post summaries there instead of (or in addition to?) relying on cron delivery
3. Document the config option in SKILL.md
4. Update the cron job delivery to point to the orchestrator channel

For this installation:
- Orchestrator channel: 1476813011925598343 (#orchestrator)
- Update the braids-orchestrator cron job delivery.to to this channel ID

Note: The cron job currently delivers to #braids (1471680593497554998). Update it to deliver to #orchestrator (1476813011925598343).

title: Comprehensive braids CLI cleanup: simplify commands, improve UX, remove legacy features, update documentation
description:
Clean up the braids CLI for better usability and maintainability:

1. Remove orch-tick command (redundant with orch --dry-run)
2. Update orch help text: 'Run orchestrator: compute spawns, start workers (defaults to dry run)'
3. Enable --help on each sub-command for better documentation
4. Remove JSON output from orch - use human-readable stdout only
5. orch writes to stdout instead of stderr/log files
6. orch logs what it finds and actions it will take (verbose stdout)
7. Remove disable logic from orch (simplify)
8. System cron appends orch output to /tmp/braids.log
9. Update all skill documents and documentation
10. Evaluate list command - keep if useful, remove if redundant

Benefits:
- Simpler, more intuitive CLI
- Better observability with stdout logging
- No accidental side effects (dry-run default)
- Cleaner codebase without legacy complexity
- Comprehensive documentation updates

title: Fix gherkin_spec.clj file path — move to spec/braids/ to match namespace
description: spec/gherkin_spec.clj declares namespace braids.gherkin-spec but lives at the top level instead of spec/braids/. Move it to spec/braids/gherkin_spec.clj to match the namespace convention used by all other braids.*-spec files.

title: Rewrite orchestrator.md to use braids CLI
description: Slim orchestrator.md from the current multi-page doc to: run braids orch tick, spawn what it says. Remove backoff logic, file parsing, frequency scaling — all now in CLI. The orchestrator agent becomes a thin wrapper.

title: CONTRACTS.md: document invariants for orchestrator, worker, and file formats
braids init: first-time setup
description: First-time setup command. Creates ~/.openclaw/braids/ directory, initializes registry.edn, sets up orchestrator cron. Replaces init.md reference doc.

title: Spec: zombie detection — scenarios for detecting and cleaning up stale worker sessions
description:
Write Gherkin scenarios for zombie session detection.

Cover:
- Given a session labeled braids-<bead>-worker exists AND bead is closed → session is zombie
- Given a session has exceeded worker-timeout → session is zombie
- Given a zombie session → it is killed and NOT counted toward active workers
- Given a session exists AND bead is in_progress → session is NOT a zombie
- Given a bead is in_progress but NO session exists → bead should be reopened

File: spec/features/zombie_detection.feature

title: Build thin Gherkin runner in Babashka — parse .feature files, map steps to Clojure step defs, run as 'bb features'
description:
## Goal
Build a lightweight Gherkin feature runner for the braids project.

## Requirements
- Parse .feature files from spec/features/
- Support Feature:, Background:, Scenario:, Given/When/Then/And/But steps
- Map steps to Clojure step definition functions via regex matching
- Run all features with: bb features (separate from bb test)
- Report pass/fail per scenario with clear output
- Exit code 1 if any scenario fails, 0 if all pass

## File structure
- spec/features/*.feature — feature files (written by prowl)
- spec/step_defs/*.clj — step definition files (one per feature or domain)
- src/braids/gherkin.clj — thin parser
- bb.edn — add features task

## Non-goals
- Full Gherkin compliance (no doc strings, no data tables for now)
- No external dependencies

title: Fix verbose mode: post step-by-step updates during tick, not just a summary at the end
description:
Current verbose mode posts a summary after the tick completes. This is not useful — by the time you see it, the work is done or timed out.

Fix: When verbose is enabled, post to :orchestrator-channel at each step as it happens:

Step 1 — tick start:
'🤖 Orchestrator tick started'

Step 2 — after sessions_list:
'📋 Sessions: project:zaap:8kq (running), project:braids:xyz | Passing to CLI...'

Step 3 — after braids orch-run:
'⚙️ orch-run result: spawn 2 workers [zaap-8kq, braids-dwc]'
OR
'⚙️ orch-run result: idle (no-ready-beads) → staying alive'
OR
'⚙️ orch-run result: idle (no-active-iterations) → disabling cron'

Step 4 — after each sessions_spawn:
'🏗️ Spawning scrapper for zaap-8kq...'
'✅ Worker spawned: project:zaap:zaap-8kq'

Step 5 — tick complete:
'✅ Tick complete'

Each message is posted immediately as the step completes, giving real-time visibility.

Update orchestrator.md with these step-by-step posting instructions.

title: Remove RETRO.md auto-generation feature — redundant with deliverables and bd list

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Remove RETRO.md generation from worker.md steps 7-8. Remove the .completing lock file mechanism (only existed for RETRO coordination). Remove RETRO contract tests and specs. Simplify iteration completion to: check no open beads, mark ITERATION.md status complete, notify channel. Existing RETRO.md files in completed iterations can stay as historical artifacts but no new ones should be generated.

title: Rename projects-home to braids-home
acceptance_criteria: All references to projects-home renamed to braids-home

title: Fix SKILL.md quick reference: close command is 'bd close <id>' not 'bd update <id> --close'
description:
The SKILL.md Beads Quick Reference documents the wrong close command:

WRONG: bd update <id> --close
RIGHT: bd close <id>

Workers have been failing to close beads because --close is not a valid flag for bd update. Fix the quick reference in SKILL.md and verify references/worker.md uses the correct syntax too.

title: Add README.md with installation instructions
description: Explain how to install the skill: symlink to ~/.openclaw/skills, install beads, etc.

title: Remove phantom test/ path from bb.edn
description: bb.edn includes "test" in :paths but the test/ directory does not exist. Either remove it from :paths or create the directory if it is intended for future use. Removing is preferred — the project uses spec/ for all tests.

title: Update braids CLI to support 'help <command>' syntax by routing to '<command> --help'
Add colored stderr debug output to braids orch-run
description:
Add human-readable debug output to stderr when running 'braids orch-run' (stdout stays clean JSON for orchestrator parsing).

Format:
- One line per project showing: name, status, active iteration (or 'no iteration')
- For projects with active iterations: indented list of each bead with its status
- Final line: the decision (action, reason, disable_cron)

Color coding:
- Project name: bold white
- 'active' status: green
- 'paused'/'blocked' status: red
- Iteration number: cyan
- Bead status colors:
  - open/in-progress: green
  - blocked: red/yellow
  - closed: dim/gray
- Final decision line: green for spawn, yellow for idle-stay-alive, red for idle-disable

Example output (stderr):
  braids         active  iteration 017  → 7/7 closed ✓
  zane-setup     active  iteration 001  → 4 beads
    juw  open
    s1y  open
  zaap           active  iteration 004  → 1 blocked
    zaap-d1o  🚫 blocked
  wealth         active  (no iteration)

  → idle: no-ready-beads  [disable_cron: false]

Use ANSI color codes (same pattern as braids list command). Respect NO_COLOR env var to disable colors.

title: Move orphan top-level spec files into spec/braids/ with proper namespaces
description: 11 spec files sit directly in spec/ without the braids/ subdirectory. Most use flat namespaces (e.g. contracts-spec, simulation-spec) instead of braids.*-spec. Move them into spec/braids/ and update their ns declarations to use the braids.* prefix for consistency. Files: contracts_spec.clj, init_reference_spec.clj, install_spec.clj, integration_smoke_spec.clj, orch_runner_spec.clj, orch_shell_spec.clj, project_creation_reference_spec.clj, rename_spec.clj, simulation_spec.clj, structural_spec.clj. (gherkin_spec.clj covered by separate bead.)

title: Convert shell script orchestrator to Babashka with tests, integrate into CLI
description:
Replace the shell script orchestrator with a Babashka (bb) script for better maintainability and testing.

Requirements:
1. Convert braids-orch.sh functionality to bb script
2. Add comprehensive tests for spawn logic, JSON parsing, error handling
3. Integrate as CLI command: braids orch (replacing orch-tick)
4. Maintain same interface: call orch-tick, parse spawns, fire-and-forget via openclaw agent &
5. Add --dry-run and --verbose flags
6. Remove the shell script approach entirely

Benefits:
- Better error handling and maintainability than shell script
- Proper Clojure testing framework integration
- Consistent with existing braids codebase
- Still zero LLM overhead for orchestration

title: Homebrew formula for braids
acceptance_criteria: brew install slagyr/tap/braids works, installs bb and bd as deps, braids is on PATH. README updated with brew install as primary method plus manual install steps for non-Mac users.

title: Clean up braids orch output format: remove timestamps, banner dry-run mode, remove disable_cron, eliminate duplicate messages
description:
Refine the braids orch command output for cleaner, more focused display:

Current output issues:
- Unnecessary timestamps clutter the output
- Dry-run mode mentioned at bottom instead of prominent banner
- disable_cron info not needed in user output  
- Duplicate 'Idle: all-at-capacity' messages

Required changes:
1. Remove all timestamps from output
2. Print DRY-RUN/CONFIRMED banner at very top
3. Remove disable_cron field from output
4. Remove duplicate idle messages
5. Keep essential info: project status, bead counts, decisions

Result: Clean, focused output that clearly shows mode and actions without noise.

title: REVIEW.md only for blockers/questions, not progress summaries
description: REVIEW.md should only contain blockers and questions for the customer. If everything went smoothly, no REVIEW.md. Progress is captured in bead summaries.

title: Audit and tighten scrapper's AGENTS.md to enforce: (1) mandatory read of project AGENTS.md before any work, (2) mandatory load of TDD skill when project requires it, (3) mandatory test run before closing — workers must not close a bead without showing bb test output in the deliverable. File: ~/.openclaw/agents/scrapper/agent/AGENTS.md
Convert registry.md to registry.edn
acceptance_criteria: registry.md replaced by registry.edn in ~/.openclaw/braids/. CLI reads/writes only registry.edn. No markdown fallback.

title: bd init integration — auto-generate AGENTS.md, directory structure, and registry entry

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Already fixed. Root cause: sessions_spawn had no cleanup param, so every completed worker lingered as a zombie. Added cleanup: delete to orchestrator.md spawn call. Zombie detection remains as safety net for abnormal exits.

title: CLI: perform zombie detection and include in output
description:
braids orch-run should detect zombie sessions internally (using session data it collects) and include them in the JSON output alongside spawn decisions.

Updated output shape:
{
  'action': 'spawn' | 'idle',
  'spawns': [...],
  'zombies': [{'sessionId': '...', 'label': '...', 'reason': 'bead-closed|timeout'}],
  'disable_cron': true|false
}

The orchestrator agent then handles both: calls sessions_spawn for spawns, kills zombie sessions.
Zombie kill is still an agent tool call (no CLI command exists for it).

title: Support skill migration command via project channel
description: When the skill format updates, the user should be able to tell a project (via its channel) something like 'update to latest skill format' and the worker will: 1) Read the current SKILL.md for the latest PROJECT.md/ITERATION.md format, 2) Compare with the project's existing files, 3) Create beads to migrate any outdated files. This is a user-triggered migration path, not automatic.

title: Test end-to-end: create story, spawn sub-agent, produce deliverable
Fix worker announcement format: prefix all notifications with 'Braids worker <agent>|<model>|<bead>: ' after the emoji
description:
## Desired Format
Every worker notification message should have this prefix (after the leading emoji):

`Braids worker <agent>|<model>|<bead>: <message>`

Examples:
- `✅ Braids worker scrapper|claude-sonnet-4-6|braids-i4i: bead complete`
- `🚫 Braids worker scrapper|claude-sonnet-4-6|braids-t3m: blocker — can't find config file`
- `❓ Braids worker keaton|ollama/llama3.2|braids-w9g: question — which approach?`

## Where to fix
- Update the worker notification format in references/worker.md
- Update any announcement templates in the worker spawn code
- The agent, model, and bead values should be passed to the worker at spawn time so it can include them in every notification

title: Improve orchestrator output: for each project line, show active workers / max workers and list beads that are in-progress or blocked. Format: 'braids active iteration:026 workers:1/3 beads:' followed by bead status lines (○ open, ● in-progress, ✗ blocked). Currently only open beads are shown.
Cron should prioritize iteration story order, then bead priority
description: When picking work, follow the order stories are listed in ITERATION.md first, fall back to bd priority for unlisted beads.

title: Fix failing unit tests in spec/braids/
description: Running bb test against spec/braids/ shows 8 test failures. Investigate each failure, determine root cause, and fix. The failures are in the core unit test suite — these must all pass. Run: bb -e '(require (quote speclj.main)) (apply speclj.main/-main ["-c" "spec/braids" "-s" "src"])' to reproduce.

title: Fix PROJECT.md settings rendering — use markdown list or double-space line breaks
description: Settings in PROJECT.md render as one line in markdown because single line breaks are ignored. Use a bullet list, a table, or trailing double-spaces to ensure one-per-line rendering.

title: Add verbose mode to orchestrator: announce reasoning to channel on every tick
description:
Add verbose mode to orchestrator: announce reasoning to channel on every tick.

When verbose mode is enabled, the orchestrator should post a detailed summary to the orchestrator channel on every run:

---
🤖 Orchestrator tick — 07:31 MST

**Sessions:** project:zaap:abc (running), project:braids:xyz (zombie - bead closed)

**CLI input:** braids orch-run --sessions 'project:zaap:abc'

**CLI output:**
```json
{"action": "spawn", "spawns": [...]}
```

**Decision:** Spawning 2 workers → zaap-47b, braids-dwc
---

Configuration:
- Add :verbose true/false to ~/.openclaw/braids/config.edn (global braids config)
- When verbose, orchestrator posts to :orchestrator-channel before and after CLI call
- Default: false

After implementing, set :verbose true in ~/.openclaw/braids/config.edn to leave verbose mode ON.

Update orchestrator.md with the verbose mode instructions and config option.

title: Debug orchestrator agent behavior
acceptance_criteria: Investigate why orchestrator agent doesn't spawn workers despite orch-tick showing spawns and updated orchestrator.md instructions. Add debugging/logging to see what the agent is actually doing vs what it should do. Fix the actual execution problem.

title: Add note to worker.md clarifying skill paths: user-installed skills live in ~/.openclaw/skills/ (e.g. /Users/zane/.openclaw/skills/braids/SKILL.md), NOT ~/openclaw/skills/ (which is the OpenClaw source repo). Workers should use the full absolute path when editing skill files. File to update: /Users/zane/.openclaw/skills/braids/references/worker.md
Remove --deliver from worker spawn args — causes channel spam and routes workers into Discord session context
description:
## Problem
orch_runner.clj includes --deliver --reply-channel discord --reply-to <channel> on every worker spawn. This causes two critical bugs:

1. Every worker response is auto-delivered to the Discord channel (spam)
2. The worker session gets associated with the Discord channel context, so agents apply group-chat silence rules and respond NO_REPLY to the task message — workers never start

## Fix
Remove --deliver, --reply-channel, and --reply-to from base-args in build-worker-args.
Workers should only reach Discord via explicit message tool calls for defined notification events (bead-start, bead-complete, blocker, question, etc.) — not via automatic delivery.

## Acceptance
- Workers spawn without --deliver flag
- No automatic channel output from workers
- Workers respond to task messages (no longer NO_REPLY)
- Notification events still work via explicit message calls

title: Specs must not assume any projects exist
description: structural_spec and integration_smoke_spec read ~/Projects/registry.md and validate all registered projects. These fail on a fresh clone with no projects. Environment-dependent specs (symlink validation, registry checks, cross-project checks) should either be skipped when no registry exists or use temp fixtures like simulation_spec does.

title: Add 'planning' status for iterations
description: Iterations need a 'planning' status between creation and activation. Workers should only pick up beads from 'active' iterations, never 'planning' or 'complete'. This prevents work from starting before planning is finished. Update SKILL.md and worker.md references.

title: Clarify path conventions: ~ means user home, not agent workspace; add PROJECTS_HOME

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Already fixed ad-hoc. Added Path Convention section to SKILL.md, introduced PROJECTS_HOME (default ~/Projects), updated all references in SKILL.md and orchestrator.md, fixed registry.md paths from ~/projects/ to ~/Projects/.

title: Add comprehensive integration tests
acceptance_criteria: Integration test suite that creates real projects, iterations, and beads, then runs all CLI commands to verify they work properly. Tests actual end-to-end workflows, not just isolated functions. Catches bugs like the iteration display issue.

title: Capture stdout/stderr in tests — wrap uncaptured core/run calls with with-out-str
description: Multiple spec files call core/run without capturing output, causing help text, error messages, and config output to pollute the test run. Files with uncaptured output: spec/braids/core_spec.clj (lines 76, 80), spec/braids/config_command_spec.clj (lines 58, 63), spec/braids/integration_spec.clj (lines 341, 344, 347, 350). Wrap all core/run calls in (with-out-str ...) like the existing examples in config_command_spec.clj. Goal: bb test produces only dots and failure messages — zero stdout noise.

title: Add MaxAgents to PROJECT.md, cron respects running agent count
description: PROJECT.md gets a MaxAgents field (default 1). Cron should check how many agents are currently working on a project and only spawn more if under the limit.

title: Add runTimeoutSeconds to worker spawn to hard-kill long-running sessions
Add braids config command set
acceptance_criteria: braids config get/set/list commands for reading and updating config

title: Replace homegrown test framework with actual speclj — use the real library

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Worker built a 'speclj-inspired' mini framework in spec_helper.clj instead of using the actual speclj library. Replace with real speclj: add speclj dep to bb.edn, rewrite specs to use (describe ...) (it ...) (should ...) syntax, remove spec_helper.clj. speclj has native Babashka support. See https://github.com/slagyr/speclj for setup.

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Use speclj 3.12.0 (latest on Clojars). bb.edn dep: speclj/speclj {:mvn/version "3.12.0"}

title: Sideline blocking/hanging specs — mark pending or move to integration suite
description: Several top-level spec files shell out to external tools (bd, openclaw, git) and hang or fail when those tools are unavailable or slow. These specs block bb test from completing. Specs to sideline: simulation_spec.clj, orch_shell_spec.clj, structural_spec.clj, integration_smoke_spec.clj. Options: mark all tests in these files as (pending) with a note, or move them to a separate bb test:integration task. Also fix simulation_spec.clj top-level side effects (line 291 rm -rf at load time, line 11 slurp at load time). Goal: bb test completes in seconds with no hangs.

title: Standardize iteration status format and add orchestrator format tolerance

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Workers reformatted iteration 006 status from 'Status: active' to '- **Status:** active' (markdown bold + list prefix). The orchestrator failed to recognize this as active for 12+ hours, burning tokens on idle backoff cycles. Fix: (1) Document the exact required format in orchestrator.md and worker.md, (2) Add a speclj spec that validates iteration status format, (3) Consider making the orchestrator instructions more explicit about format matching.

title: Orchestrator: use --session-id when spawning workers so worker sessions are trackable across cron ticks
description:
## Problem
The orchestrator spawns workers via `openclaw agent --agent <agent> --message <task>` but doesn't set a session ID. Each cron tick loads sessions fresh and can't find the previously spawned worker session — so workers=0 every tick and the same bead gets re-spawned indefinitely.

## Fix
Generate a deterministic session ID per bead (e.g. `braids-<bead-id>-worker`) and pass it via `--session-id` when invoking `openclaw agent`. On subsequent ticks, the orchestrator checks for that session ID to determine if a worker is already running for that bead.

## Acceptance
- Orchestrator spawns worker with `--session-id braids-<bead-id>-worker`
- Next tick correctly detects the running worker session and does not re-spawn
- Zombie detection still works (session ended = no longer active)

title: Move PROJECT.md and iterations/ into .project/ directory to reduce root clutter

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Move PROJECT.md and iterations/ into .project/ so the project root stays clean for actual project content. AGENTS.md stays at root (agent entry point convention). Update all references in SKILL.md, worker.md, orchestrator.md, CONTRACTS.md, project-creation.md, migration.md, and specs. This is a breaking change — existing projects will need migration. Document the migration path. This project (projects-skill) should be migrated as part of the bead.

title: Add :worker-thinking config field to project config — default to 'high'; replaces hardcoded 'low' in orch_runner.clj
Canary: sleep 60 seconds then report
description: Busywork bead for testing orchestrator behavior. Worker should sleep for 60 seconds (using 'sleep 60' in exec), then write a deliverable confirming it completed, and close the bead. No real work — just simulating a long-running task.

title: Spec: orchestrator spawning behavior — scenarios for tick decisions, spawn conditions, idle conditions
description:
Write Gherkin scenarios for the braids orchestrator spawning logic.

Cover:
- Given an active iteration with open beads and no active workers → spawn a worker
- Given max-workers already reached → idle: all-at-capacity
- Given no active iteration → idle: no-active-iterations
- Given all beads in iteration are closed → idle: no-ready-beads
- Given a bead is blocked → do not spawn for that bead
- Given multiple projects → spawn up to max-workers per project independently

File: spec/features/orchestrator_spawning.feature

title: Move worker prompt out of CLI and into orchestrator.md
description:
The braids CLI (orch.clj) currently hardcodes the worker prompt in worker-instruction and includes it in the orch-run JSON output. This is the wrong responsibility — the CLI should not know about prompt content.

Fix:
1. Remove worker-instruction and format-spawn-msg from orch.clj
2. orch-run spawn entries should only include the structural data needed: project path, bead id, iteration, channel, label, agentId, timeouts
3. The orchestrator.md should define the worker prompt template and instruct the orchestrator agent to construct the task message itself before calling sessions_spawn

The orchestrator already reads orchestrator.md and knows how to spawn — it should own the prompt content, not the CLI.

Updated spawn entry from CLI (no task field):
{
  'project': '~/Projects/braids',
  'bead': 'braids-abc',
  'iteration': '018',
  'channel': '1471680593497554998',
  'label': 'project:braids:braids-abc',
  'agentId': 'scrapper',
  'runTimeoutSeconds': 3600,
  'cleanup': 'delete',
  'thinking': 'low'
}

orchestrator.md defines the task template and the agent fills it in.

title: Standardize terminology: use 'worker' not 'agent' throughout docs
description: Replace all references to 'agent' (when referring to spawned sessions doing project work) with 'worker'. Update SKILL.md, references/worker.md, PROJECT.md format (MaxAgents -> MaxWorkers), and any other docs. 'Worker' = an isolated session spawned to execute project tasks.

title: Migrate iterations/ to .project/iterations/ for projects-skill
description: ly3 moved PROJECT.md to .project/ but iterations/ is still at project root. Move iterations/ into .project/iterations/ and update any hardcoded paths in specs or references.

title: Fix Homebrew braids path resolution
acceptance_criteria: Homebrew-installed braids finds active iterations and ready beads correctly. Fix path resolution issues that cause 'No active iteration found' and 'No ready beads' when iterations and beads exist. Ensure consistency with development version behavior.

title: Sideline gherkin tests — separate bb test:gherkin task, exclude from bb test
description: The gherkin test infrastructure (gherkin.clj, gherkin_runner.clj, gherkin_spec.clj, spec/features/, spec/step_defs/) is incomplete and breaks the main test suite. Sideline it: 1) Add a bb test:gherkin task in bb.edn for running gherkin specs separately. 2) Exclude gherkin_spec.clj from the main bb test run (move it out of the spec path or use speclj tags). 3) Ensure bb test no longer loads gherkin-related namespaces. The gherkin code stays in src/ — we just decouple it from the default test run.

title: Fix orchestrator self-disable: stay enabled while active iterations exist
description:
The orchestrator currently disables itself whenever there are no ready beads (bd ready returns empty). This is too aggressive — it should only disable when there are no active iterations across all projects.

The correct logic:
- Disable: no active iterations exist (nothing could ever become ready)
- Stay enabled: active iterations exist, even if no beads are ready right now (beads may become unblocked)

The orch-tick/orch-run CLI output includes the disable_cron flag. This flag should only be true when there are truly no active iterations, not just when no beads are currently ready.

Fix in src/braids/orch.clj — the idle reason 'no-ready-beads' should NOT trigger disable_cron. Only 'no-active-iterations' should.

title: Validate multi-project support with concurrent active projects
Remove Budget from PROJECT.md format and all docs
description: Budget can't be meaningfully enforced right now — no reliable cumulative cost/token tracking available. Remove Budget field from PROJECT.md format in SKILL.md, PROJECT.md itself, and any references.

title: INIT.md: one-time skill setup reference — install skill, create PROJECTS_HOME, set up cron, first project

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Create references/init.md — a one-time setup guide an agent follows when a user says 'set up the projects skill' or similar. Steps: (1) install/symlink skill to ~/.openclaw/skills/projects, (2) verify bd is installed, (3) create PROJECTS_HOME (~/Projects) and registry.md, (4) set up orchestrator cron job with recommended config, (5) optionally create first project via projects-init. Also update README.md to point to INIT.md as the automated alternative to manual setup. Update SKILL.md installation section similarly.

title: Data format migration: registry.edn and project.edn schemas
description: Define EDN schemas for registry (replaces registry.md table) and project config (replaces PROJECT.md config fields). Write migration tool from markdown to EDN. CLI reads structured data from day one. PROJECT.md narrative sections (Goal, Guardrails) stay as markdown or fold into AGENTS.md.

title: Add notification rules to SKILL.md
description: Send notifications to project channel when: all iteration stories complete, no ready beads, questions arise, blockers arise.

title: Workers must generate RETRO.md before marking iteration complete

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Worker.md steps 7-8 require generating RETRO.md when an iteration completes, but iteration 004 completed without one. The worker either skipped the step or the race condition meant the 'winning' worker didn't follow through. This may be related to um4 (duplicate notifications). Ensure the worker that marks the iteration complete also generates the retro, and add a contract test: completed iterations must have RETRO.md.

title: Structural test script: validate files, symlinks, registry, and spawn config
Colorize braids ready output
acceptance_criteria: braids ready uses colors: priority badges (P1=red, P2=yellow, P3=green), bead titles (bold white), type/status indicators colored appropriately. Maintains readability while highlighting important info.

title: Colorize braids help output
acceptance_criteria: braids help uses colors: title (bold white), usage (bold cyan), section headers (bold yellow), command names (bold blue), descriptions (white). Clean readable layout with consistent color scheme.

title: Remove SKILL.md runtime dependency from orchestrator and worker
braids orch tick: orchestrator decision engine
description: The big one. Scans all projects, finds ready beads, applies concurrency rules, returns JSON: spawn these beads or idle. Replaces 80% of orchestrator.md logic. No backoff needed — CLI is zero-cost to run. Depends on ready command for shared logic.

title: Workers should include agent ID and model in their announcements (bead-start, bead-complete, blocker, question notifications) so it's clear which agent/model did the work
Integration smoke tests: end-to-end validation after each iteration
Orchestrator should print a closing banner with timestamp when it exits, mirroring the opening banner. This makes it easy to see start/end time in logs.
Display project settings as a list in PROJECT.md, not single line
description: PROJECT.md settings (Status, Priority, Budget, etc.) should each be on their own line, not crammed together.

title: Delete tests/ directory — replaced by spec/ with speclj
Fix orchestrator spawning logic
acceptance_criteria: Orchestrator properly spawns workers from braids orch-tick results. For each entry in spawns array, calls sessions_spawn with correct parameters. Zaap and other project workers get spawned automatically. Test with real spawn results.

title: Add guardrail: channel agent must not modify project files directly — beads only

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: The channel/main session agent should only create beads, plan iterations, activate iterations, and review deliverables. It must NOT edit project files (SKILL.md, worker.md, orchestrator.md, PROJECT.md, CONTRACTS.md, etc.) directly. All file changes go through beads assigned to workers. Add this as a guardrail in PROJECT.md and document in SKILL.md as a project channel convention.

title: Orchestrator frequency scaling — reduce polling when no active iterations exist
Rename .project directory to .braids
acceptance_criteria: All projects use .braids/ instead of .project/. All references in skill docs, CLI code, worker.md, orchestrator.md, and specs updated.

title: Prevent duplicate iteration-complete notifications — guard against race condition

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: When multiple workers finish beads around the same time, each checks bd ready, sees no open beads, and sends an iteration-complete notification. Fix: worker should check ITERATION.md status before announcing — if it's already 'complete', skip the notification. The first worker to mark it complete wins; others see it's already done and stay quiet.

title: Support @mention in iteration-complete notifications for phone alerts

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Already fixed. Added mention syntax to iteration-complete notification in PROJECT.md. Worker includes the mention when sending the completion message to the channel, triggering a phone notification.

title: Cron worker is orchestrator only — spawns workers, does no bead work
description: The cron worker should ONLY: 1) read registry, 2) check active iterations, 3) count running workers vs MaxWorkers, 4) spawn async sessions (sessions_spawn) for beads, 5) sleep. It must NOT do any bead work itself. All bead work happens in spawned worker sessions. Update worker.md and cron prompt.

title: braids status: dashboard view
description: Show dashboard across all projects or single project detail. Replaces STATUS.md generation. Shows active iterations, bead progress, worker state. Human-friendly output + --json.

title: Optimize orchestrator: reduce zombie detection overhead
description:
The orchestrator is timing out (300s) because zombie detection is expensive — it calls 'bd show <id>' for every active session label to check if the bead is closed. With multiple projects and sessions this blows the time budget before any workers get spawned.

Fix options:
1. Make zombie detection optional or lazy — skip it if sessions_list shows no project: labeled sessions
2. Batch the bead status checks (bd list instead of individual bd show calls)
3. Move zombie detection to a separate lightweight pass
4. Simplify criteria — only check session status (completed/failed/stopped), skip the bead-closed check

Goal: orchestrator should complete in under 60s even with many active projects.

title: Add :path and binary config to global braids config (~/.openclaw/braids/config.edn): :env-path (prepended to subprocess PATH), :bd-bin (default 'bd'), :openclaw-bin (default 'openclaw'). All subprocess calls use the configured bins; PATH config ensures cron and other minimal-PATH environments work correctly.
Remove STATUS.md dashboard generation
acceptance_criteria: Remove STATUS.md file generation from orchestrator. Delete references/status-dashboard.md. Remove Step 5 from orchestrator.md. Keep 'braids status' CLI command for live queries. Clean up STATUS.md references in docs.

title: Extract worker orchestration into projects/references/worker.md
description: Move all orchestration logic (registry check, agent counting, MaxAgents enforcement, work prioritization, spawning, notifications) from the cron prompt into projects/references/worker.md. Cron prompt should simply say 'read and follow projects/references/worker.md'. Single source of truth for the worker strategy.

title: Add Discord channel creation for project check-ins
braids ready: list beads ready to work
description: Core logic command. Lists beads that are open, have deps met, and project not at worker capacity. Shared logic with orch tick. This is the heart of the orchestrator decision engine extracted into deterministic code.

title: Workers must read PROJECT.md on startup for guardrails and config
description: Spawned worker sessions should read PROJECT.md as their first step to pick up guardrails, autonomy level, and other project config. Update worker.md to specify this.
notes: Workers must also read AGENTS.md on startup, not just PROJECT.md.

title: Migrate existing projects from PROJECT.md to project.edn
acceptance_criteria: braids migrate converts PROJECT.md to project.edn in all registered projects. Old PROJECT.md files removed after migration.

title: Replace LLM orchestrator with shell script for true fire-and-forget spawning
description:
The LLM agent orchestrator times out because it can't truly fire-and-forget — it waits for tool call responses. Replace it with a shell script that:

1. Calls braids orch-tick to get spawn decisions
2. For each spawn, calls openclaw agent --agent <id> --message <task> --session-id <uuid> --deliver &
3. Exits immediately after spawning all workers

This eliminates LLM reasoning overhead and provides true fire-and-forget spawning with no timeouts.

Update the cron job to run the shell script instead of the agent turn.

title: Tighten worker spawns to prevent zombies
acceptance_criteria: Workers reliably terminate after completing or failing a bead. No more zombie sessions lingering. Shorter default timeouts, worker instructions updated to exit cleanly, orchestrator spawn parameters optimized.

title: Formalize worker error handling and escalation path
Simplify README.md install: clone repo + symlink, remove manual setup steps

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Current README has too many manual steps. Simplify to: (1) git clone the repo, (2) symlink projects/ into ~/.openclaw/skills/projects. Two commands. Remove all the manual mkdir, cat, registry creation steps — those are handled by the agent-driven project-creation.md flow or INIT.md. README should be concise: what this is, how to install (2 steps), link to SKILL.md for details.

title: Add cleanup: delete to worker spawn to prevent zombie sessions by default

comments.author: micahmartin
comments.text: Already fixed. Root cause: sessions_spawn had no cleanup param, so every completed worker lingered as a zombie. Added cleanup: delete to orchestrator.md spawn call. Zombie detection remains as safety net for abnormal exits.

title: Rename orch_runner_test.clj to orch_runner_spec.clj — consistent naming
description: spec/braids/orch_runner_test.clj uses the -test suffix (braids.orch-runner-test) while every other spec file uses -spec. Rename to orch_runner_spec.clj and update the namespace to braids.orch-runner-spec. Note: there is also a separate top-level spec/orch_runner_spec.clj (orch-runner-spec) — reconcile or merge these two files as appropriate.
